title: STDIO, Streamable HTTP, Lifecycle, and Deployment · Skill Foundry
summary: Choose and configure a transport for local and remote MCP servers. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a local and remote transport deployment plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement newline-safe STDIO transport without logging protocol noise to standard output.
Implement remote Streamable HTTP with correct routing, content types, and transport security.
Handle startup, initialization, shutdown, timeout, and reconnect behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Observability condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Observability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Log identifiers, latency, errors, and side effects without leaking protocol secrets.
